title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
summary: The piece argues that small agile teams are more productive and efficient than large teams, which become bogged down in excessive meetings. It advocates for smaller team units as a superior organizational structure.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof contends that small agile teams outperform large ones on productivity and efficiency, with larger groups slowed by excessive meetings. The argument frames smaller team units as the superior organizational structure. The core takeaway: meeting overhead scales with team size, and keeping teams small limits that drag on output.
